item.title: Construction begins for UFC fight on White House lawn
item.description: Construction is underway at the White House for a UFC cage match on President Trump's birthday in June. Political strategists Kendra Barkoff Lamy and Terry Sullivan join to discuss.

item.title: Trump undergoes "6 month physical" at Walter Reed
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday underwent a "6 month physical" at Walter Reed National Military Hospital, he posted on social media.

item.title: Trump has third medical exam of the past year
item.description: President Trump said his health "checked out perfectly" after a medical checkup on Tuesday. Nancy Cordes reports.

item.title: DHS memo directs ICE to ramp up asylum-related fraud cases
item.description: A top DHS official directed ICE attorneys to aggressively pursue administrative fraud cases against immigration lawyers accused of filing false asylum claims.

item.title: Deaths reported after chemical tank implodes at Washington mill
item.description: Nippon Dynawave Packaging Co. and local law enforcement said the tank's rupture caused "multiple critical injuries" as well as fatalities.

item.title: Police investigating deadly Michigan home explosion
item.description: In western Michigan, an overnight explosion destroyed a home and a car in a driveway. Police said one man was found dead, and a neighbor suffered burns from helping a woman who is now in critical condition. Ash-har Quraishi reports from the scene and has doorbell camera footage.

item.title: DHS directs ICE to ramp up asylum fraud cases against immigration lawyers, memo shows
item.description: According to a memo obtained by CBS News, the Department of Homeland Security is directing attorneys who work with ICE to pursue fraud cases against immigration lawyers. CBS News homeland security correspondent Nicole Sganga has the exclusive.

item.title: Chemical blast in Washington state kills multiple people, authorities say
item.description: Authorities in Longview, Washington, responded to a chemical tank blast Tuesday in what first responders called a "mass casualty event." It involved a vat of chemicals used to turn wood pulp into tissues, wrapping paper and other packaging. Jonathan Vigliotti reports.

item.title: Chemical tank implodes in Washington state, authorities report deaths
item.description: Authorities have confirmed an undisclosed number of deaths and at least 10 injuries following the implosion of a chemical tank at a pulp and paper mill in Washington state. CBS News correspondent Jonathan Vigliotti reports.

item.title: Breaking down the decision as court blocks GOP-backed Alabama redistricting plan
item.description: A federal court has blocked a congressional redistricting plan in Alabama, finding that the map intentionally discriminated against Black voters. Republicans say they'll appeal the decision to the Supreme Court. CBS News chief legal correspondent Jan Crawford breaks down the case.

item.title: Alleged gunman outside White House had run-ins with Secret Service, sources say
item.description: Sources identified the 21-year-old suspect as Nasire Best of Dundalk, Maryland, and documents obtained by CBS News show Best previously blocked a White House entry lane in June 2025.

item.title: What's at stake in race between John Cornyn and Ken Paxton in Texas?
item.description: Texas voters went to the polls on Tuesday for the Republican U.S. Senate primary runoff between incumbent Sen. John Cornyn and Trump-backed Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton. CBS News political director Fin Gómez reports.

item.title: 2 Navy pilots safely eject after training jet crashes in Mississippi
item.description: The T-45C Goshawk, a tandem seat aircraft designed to train Navy and Marine Corps pilots, crashed on private farmland in Noxubee County, Mississippi.

item.title: Where do peace negotiations with Iran stand?
item.description: The U.S. launched "self-defense strikes" overnight in Iran, which Iran called a ceasefire violation. Despite that, the Trump administration is still pushing for a peace deal. CBS News' Aaron MacLean has analysis and Taurean Small reports.

item.title: NASA's moon base plans include landers, buggies and drones for 2028 mission
item.description: Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin​, Astrolab, Lunar Outpost and Firefly Aerospace are awarded with hundreds of millions of dollars in NASA contracts for the first phase of its moon base plans.

item.title: Sen. Ron Johnson on potential deal with Iran: "Let's see how this all develops"
item.description: Sources say a potential U.S.-Iran deal includes a 60-day ceasefire extension, a commitment to halt fighting on every front, including in Lebanon, and assurances from Iran that it won't develop nuclear weapons. Republican Sen. Ron Johnson of Wisconsin joins "The Takeout" to weigh in on the possible agreement, the Justice Department's "anti-weaponization" fund and more.

item.title: 3 Ebola treatment centers in Congo attacked
item.description: In the last few days, three Ebola treatment centers in the Congo have been attacked. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder, an infectious disease specialist who spent time in Guinea as an aid worker during the West African Ebola epidemic, joins with the latest on the outbreak.

item.title: Here's why Trump is backing Ken Paxton over John Cornyn in Texas GOP Senate primary
item.description: Sen. John Cornyn is facing Trump-backed Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton in the Lone Star State's runoff for the Republican Senate nomination. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe reports from Austin.

item.title: Trump says physical exam went "perfectly" as details on Iran talks remain scarce
item.description: Following his third physical in 13 months on Tuesday, President Trump said his health is perfect. The exam comes a day after the U.S. launched "self-defense strikes" in southern Iran. CBS News' Nancy Cordes and Aaron MacLean have the latest.

item.title: Meet the 2026 World Cup U.S. Men's National Team
item.description: The squad was announced during an event in New York City on Tuesday after U.S. Men's National Team manager Mauricio Pochettino spent months evaluating players to finalize the roster.

item.title: Trump administration proposes NDAs for all federal employees to curb leaks
item.description: The Trump administration plans to crack down on press leaks by requiring new and existing federal employees to sign NDAs.

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Florida suit against other states over immigrant truck drivers
item.description: Florida sought to sue Washington and California for allegedly issuing commercial driver's licenses to undocumented immigrants.

item.title: Infrared camera on sailboat may hold clues in Lynette Hooker investigation
item.description: CBS News has learned the sailboat used by Brian and Lynette Hooker before her disappearance in the Bahamas had an infrared camera capable of detecting heat radiation.

item.title: U.S.-wanted nephew of drug lord "El Chapo" is captured in Mexico
item.description: The nephew of notorious Mexican drug lord Joaquin "El Chapo" Guzman is sought by U.S. authorities, officials said.

item.title: New species of blue octopus discovered 5,900 feet underwater
item.description: The new species, named Microeledone galapagensis, has a blue hue, which is believed to be the rarest color in nature.

item.title: North Korea fires ballistic missile, other weapons toward sea, South says
item.description: North Korea launched multiple short-range ballistic missiles toward the sea, South Korea's military said, the latest in a series of weapons demonstrations by Pyongyang this year.

item.title: Sonny Rollins, trailblazing jazz saxophonist, dies at age 95
item.description: Sonny Rollins, the legendary tenor saxophonist known for his bold tone and constant experimentation, has died at 95.

item.title: Wander Franco found criminally responsible for abusing minor, avoids jail time
item.description: A Dominican judge has ruled that Tampa Bay Rays shortstop Wander Franco is criminally responsible for the sexual and psychological abuse of a minor but will not serve a sentence.

item.title: Pope Leo calls for "disarming" of AI in technology-focused encyclical
item.description: In his "Magnifica Humanitas" encyclical, Pope Leo warns that as civilization grapples with the power of AI, the main challenge is remaining "profoundly human."

item.title: Inside Iran's online propaganda war
item.description: An online propaganda war is currently being waged between the United States and Iran, and global reaction suggests the Iranians are beating the Americans at their own game.
